name: World History
description: A survey of human civilizations from ancient Mesopotamia through the modern era, examining political, economic, social, and cultural developments across the globe.

modules.title: Early Humans & the Neolithic Revolution
modules.titleZh: 早期人类与新石器革命
modules.objectives: Trace human migration out of Africa; explain how agriculture transformed society.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax World History Vol 1 — Ch 1: Prehistory
modules.video2Note: Crash Course World History #1 — The Agricultural Revolution
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Early Humans reading & questions
modules.assignment: Compare hunter-gatherer life with early agricultural societies on 3 dimensions: diet, social structure, and population density.

modules.title: Ancient Mesopotamia & Egypt
modules.titleZh: 古代美索不达米亚与埃及
modules.objectives: Describe the rise of river-valley civilizations; analyze writing, law codes, and monumental architecture.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax World History Vol 1 — Ch 2: Early Civilizations
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy — Mesopotamia & the Fertile Crescent
modules.assignment: Compare Hammurabi's Code with a modern law. What does each reveal about its society's values?

modules.title: Ancient India & China
modules.objectives: Identify the key features of the Indus Valley Civilization; trace the Shang and Zhou dynasties of China.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax World History Vol 1 — Ch 3: India & China
modules.video2Note: Crash Course World History #8 — Ancient India
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Ancient India reading & questions
modules.assignment: Create a table comparing political structure, religion, and trade of early India and early China.

modules.title: Ancient Greece & the Persian Wars
modules.objectives: Explain the development of democracy in Athens; analyze the causes and consequences of the Persian Wars.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax World History Vol 1 — Ch 5: The Greeks
modules.video2Note: Crash Course World History #5 — Ancient Greece
modules.assignment: Evaluate: Was Athenian democracy truly democratic? Support your argument with specific evidence.

modules.title: The Roman Empire
modules.objectives: Trace Rome's rise from republic to empire; explain factors that led to its fall.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax World History Vol 1 — Ch 6: Rome
modules.video2Note: Crash Course World History #10 — The Roman Empire
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Roman Republic article & questions
modules.assignment: Analyze three internal and two external factors in Rome's decline. Which was most significant and why?

modules.title: The Rise of Islam & Islamic Golden Age
modules.titleZh: 伊斯兰的兴起与黄金时代
modules.objectives: Describe the origins of Islam; explain the spread of the Islamic caliphates and their cultural contributions.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax World History Vol 1 — Ch 11: Islam
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Islamic Golden Age article & questions
modules.assignment: Identify 3 scientific or mathematical contributions of the Islamic Golden Age and explain their lasting impact.

modules.title: Medieval Europe & the Feudal System
modules.titleZh: 中世纪欧洲与封建制度
modules.objectives: Explain how feudalism organized medieval society; analyze the role of the Catholic Church.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax World History Vol 1 — Ch 12: Medieval Europe
modules.video2Note: Crash Course World History #14 — The Dark Ages
modules.assignment: Draw and label a feudal pyramid. Write a paragraph explaining how each level depended on the others.

modules.title: The Mongol Empire & Global Trade
modules.objectives: Assess the impact of Mongol conquests on Eurasia; explain how the Silk Road facilitated cultural exchange.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax World History Vol 1 — Ch 13: The Mongols
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Mongol Empire article & questions
modules.assignment: Was the Mongol Empire a force for destruction or development? Write a 300-word position essay.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the Neolithic Revolution?
options: The transition from nomadic to agricultural lifestyles
The migration of humans from Africa
questions.answer: The transition from nomadic to agricultural lifestyles
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: The transition from nomadic to agricultural lifestyles.

questions.question: Mesopotamia is located between which two rivers?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Tigris and Euphrates.

questions.question: Hammurabi's Code is historically significant because it:
options: Was the first known written law code
Granted equal rights to all people
questions.answer: Was the first known written law code
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Was the first known written law code.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which civilization built the Indus Valley cities of Mohenjo-daro and Harappa" is: Harappan civilization.
questions.explanation: True. Harappan civilization is correct.

questions.question: The Mandate of Heaven was a political concept from:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Ancient China.

questions.question: Athenian democracy differed from modern democracy primarily because:
options: Citizens voted on all laws directly
Slaves and women could not participate
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Both A and B.

questions.question: The Persian Wars (490–480 BCE) were fought between:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Greece and Persia.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Rome's transition from Republic to Empire was triggered largely by:" is: An invasion from Carthage.
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Political instability and Julius Caesar's dictatorship.

questions.question: Which religion emerged from the Arabian Peninsula in the 7th century CE?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Islam.

questions.question: The Islamic Golden Age is best known for contributions to:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Mathematics, astronomy, and medicine.

questions.question: In the feudal system, lords received land (fiefs) from:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: The king or higher lords.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "The Silk Road primarily facilitated:" is: Trade and cultural exchange between East and West.
questions.explanation: True. Trade and cultural exchange between East and West is correct.

questions.question: Genghis Khan united the Mongol tribes around:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: 1200 CE.

questions.question: The Black Death (1346–1353) killed approximately what fraction of Europe's population?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: One-third.

questions.question: Which city-state was the center of the Italian Renaissance?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Florence.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Agriculture first emerged independently in multiple regions including:" is: Only the Fertile Crescent.
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: The Fertile Crescent, China, and Mesoamerica.

questions.question: Egypt's Old Kingdom is best known for:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Building pyramids at Giza.

questions.question: The caste system in ancient India was tied to:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Hindu religious beliefs.

questions.question: Alexander the Great's conquests spread which culture across the Middle East and Central Asia?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Hellenistic (Greek).

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "The Pax Romana refers to:" is: Two centuries of relative peace in the Roman Empire.
questions.explanation: True. Two centuries of relative peace in the Roman Empire is correct.

questions.question: The Five Pillars of Islam include all EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Meditation.

questions.question: Serfs in medieval Europe were different from enslaved people because:
options: They were bound to the land but not owned as property
They were free to move anywhere
questions.answer: They were bound to the land but not owned as property
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: They were bound to the land but not owned as property.

questions.question: The Mongol Empire's 'Pax Mongolica' refers to:
options: A period of relative stability enabling trade across Eurasia
questions.answer: A period of relative stability enabling trade across Eurasia
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: A period of relative stability enabling trade across Eurasia.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "The printing press, invented by Gutenberg around 1440, most directly:" is: Caused the Black Death.
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Spread ideas of the Renaissance and Reformation rapidly.

questions.question: Columbus's 1492 voyage was funded by:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Spain.

questions.question: The Middle Passage refers to:
options: A mountain route through the Alps
The sea journey enslaved Africans were forced to take to the Americas
A trade route through Central Asia
A canal connecting the Mediterranean to the Red Sea
questions.answer: The sea journey enslaved Africans were forced to take to the Americas
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: The sea journey enslaved Africans were forced to take to the Americas.

questions.question: Isaac Newton's laws of motion are a product of which intellectual movement?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: The Scientific Revolution.

questions.question: The Industrial Revolution began first in:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Britain.

questions.question: The spark that ignited World War I was:
options: The assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand
questions.answer: The assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: The assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand.

questions.question: The Treaty of Versailles (1919) contributed to WWII by:
options: Imposing harsh penalties that fueled German resentment
questions.answer: Imposing harsh penalties that fueled German resentment
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Imposing harsh penalties that fueled German resentment.

questions.question: The Holocaust refers to:
options: Nazi Germany's genocide of six million Jews and others
The Japanese attack on Pearl Harbor
questions.answer: Nazi Germany's genocide of six million Jews and others
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Nazi Germany's genocide of six million Jews and others.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Decolonization in the mid-20th century was primarily driven by:" is: Economic agreements.
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Nationalist movements demanding independence.

questions.question: The Cold War was characterized by:
options: Direct military conflict between the US and USSR
Proxy wars, arms races, and ideological competition
questions.answer: Proxy wars, arms races, and ideological competition
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Proxy wars, arms races, and ideological competition.

questions.question: Globalization in the late 20th century was accelerated by:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: The internet and containerized shipping.

questions.question: The United Nations was founded after WWII primarily to:
options: Divide the world between the US and USSR
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Promote international peace and cooperation.

quizQuestions.question: What is the Neolithic Revolution?
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Transition from hunting-gathering to farming.

quizQuestions.question: Which feature best defines a civilization?
options: Complex society with cities, government, and specialization
quizQuestions.answer: Complex society with cities, government, and specialization
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Complex society with cities, government, and specialization.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The Neolithic Revolution is "Transition from hunting-gathering to farming".
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Transition from hunting-gathering to farming is correct.

quizQuestions.question: The first civilization emerged in:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Mesopotamia (Sumer).

quizQuestions.question: Hammurabi's Code is significant because:
options: It was among the first written legal codes
quizQuestions.answer: It was among the first written legal codes
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: It was among the first written legal codes.

quizQuestions.question: The Nile River was important to ancient Egypt because:
options: It provided a trade route to Rome
Its annual floods deposited fertile silt
It was a military barrier only
quizQuestions.answer: Its annual floods deposited fertile silt
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Its annual floods deposited fertile silt.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "The first civilization emerged in:" is: Mesopotamia (Sumer).
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Mesopotamia (Sumer) is correct.

quizQuestions.question: The Indus Valley Civilization flourished along which river?
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Indus.

quizQuestions.question: Ancient China was founded along which river?
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Yellow River (Huang He).

quizQuestions.question: The Vedas are sacred texts of:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Hinduism.

quizQuestions.question: What form of government did ancient Athens develop?
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Direct democracy.

quizQuestions.question: The Battle of Marathon (490 BCE) was fought between:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Greeks and Persians.

quizQuestions.question: Sparta's society was primarily organized around:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Military training and discipline.

quizQuestions.question: Alexander the Great was a student of:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Aristotle.

quizQuestions.question: Rome's government before 27 BCE was a:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Republic.

quizQuestions.question: Julius Caesar was assassinated in:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: 44 BCE.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Rome's government before 27 BCE was a:" is: Republic.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Republic is correct.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Julius Caesar was assassinated in:" is: 100 BCE.
quizQuestions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: 44 BCE.

quizQuestions.question: Islam was founded by the Prophet Muhammad in:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Mecca.

quizQuestions.question: The Quran is Islam's:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Holy scripture revealed to Muhammad.

quizQuestions.question: The Islamic Golden Age produced advances in which fields?
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Mathematics, medicine, and philosophy.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Islam was founded by the Prophet Muhammad in:" is: Mecca.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Mecca is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Fill in the blank: Complete: In the context of the topic, ___ refers to "the word "algebra" comes from:."
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Arabic.

quizQuestions.question: In the feudal system, the lowest social class was:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Serfs.

quizQuestions.question: The Catholic Church in medieval Europe held power because:
options: It held spiritual authority over kings and peasants alike
quizQuestions.answer: It held spiritual authority over kings and peasants alike
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: It held spiritual authority over kings and peasants alike.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "In the feudal system, the lowest social class was:" is: Serfs.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Serfs is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Genghis Khan founded the Mongol Empire in approximately:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: 1206 CE.

quizQuestions.question: The Silk Road connected:
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: China to the Mediterranean.

quizQuestions.question: Kublai Khan ruled which empire?
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Mongol (Yuan Dynasty of China).

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Genghis Khan founded the Mongol Empire in approximately:" is: 1206 CE.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. 1206 CE is correct.
